run_summary: TXRH holding steady near entry with intact earnings thesis, now 16 days in. One position already open. Six new flags reviewed: BBY (OVERDONE fade), PLAB (RATIONAL miss confirmation, not fade), AVAV (OVERDONE geopolitical squeeze), DKS (RATIONAL miss, not fade), BLDR (OVERDONE momentum into headwind + re-entry after 0-day gain), ONTO (RATIONAL M&A financing read, sector-driven today). Taking small exploratory positions on BBY (post-earnings fade signature) and AVAV (defense positioning spike); BLDR rejected on re-entry (conf 3, no new info beyond sector momentum); PLAB/DKS/ONTO watched pending deeper thesis work.

entries.name: Best Buy Co., Inc.
entries.thesis: BBY printed earnings (8-K 2.02 on 5/28) and has now extended sharply over the trailing week while XLY is red on the day. A retailer with structural CE-demand headwinds and tariff exposure rallying this hard into a soft consumer tape suggests squeeze/positioning dynamics layered on a genuine beat — the magnitude of the multi-day move likely overshoots the fundamental delta. Fade candidates here typically retrace 20-30% of the post-print run.
entries.what_kills: The 8-K transcript reveals a major comp-sales acceleration or AI-PC refresh cycle commentary that justifies a structural rerate; continued sector-wide retail strength
entries.reasoning: Post-earnings multi-day extension (27.5% five-day move, 4.3% today) on a beat in a structurally weak discretionary tape (XLY red). The 8-K catalyst (5/28) has executed; the thesis is now about whether the 27.5% five-day run is positioning-driven overextension or justifiable on fundamentals. Setup: retail beat + sector weakness + high volume continuation historically resolves with 20-30% retracement within days. Confidence is 3 (OVERDONE classification), which fits exploratory tier (6% position). What kills: guide was larger than consensus suggests or AI-PC refresh cycle is real structural tailwind—both are testable via 8-K review. Small size appropriate for the fade hypothesis without high conviction on magnitude or timing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis assumes a 20-30% retracement is 'historically' probable, but provides no data on how many similar setups actually retrace versus extend further, making the fade hypothesis untestable without external research.
red_team.critique: The Haiku reasoning correctly identifies the setup (post-earnings extension in weak tape, 27.5% five-day move, 4.3% single-day pop, 2.24x volume) and acknowledges the fade thesis requires validation via 8-K review. The critical gap: the critique does not cite what the 8-K actually says about guidance magnitude or AI-PC commentary—it defers to future review rather than engaging with existing catalyst data. The exploratory 6% position size is appropriate for the confidence level (3), and the thesis is defensible as a 'test' of fade dynamics. However, the specific weakness is that without confirming guidance was 'modestly raised' (the what_confirms criterion), the bear case remains qualitative positioning logic rather than grounded in disclosed facts.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Iran missile launch headlines and sector-wide defense bid (ITA +2.2%)
entries.thesis: AVAV up ~18% today on 3.3x volume with no 8-K, riding Iran missile headlines and broader defense tape (ITA +2.2%). The stock is up ~31% in five days with no company-specific catalyst visible — that's a positioning move, not a fundamentals move. Drone/loitering-munition names get bid hard on Middle East escalation, but historically these spikes retrace 30-50% within two weeks once the headline news fades, absent an actual contract award. The five-day magnitude is now disproportionate to what's been confirmed.
entries.what_kills: An actual large Switchblade or JUMP-20 contract announcement, or material Iran escalation (strikes, Strait closure) — either would justify the move and convert it to UNDERDONE
entries.reasoning: 18% single-day pop (+30.8% five-day) on Iran missile headlines and sector bid (no 8-K, no contract award). Defense mid-caps historically retrace 30-50% of geopolitical spikes within two weeks absent contract confirmation or material escalation. Setup: positioning-driven rally on headline without fundamental catalyst. Volume (3.28x) and magnitude (31% five-day) are consistent with squeeze rather than earnings/contract breakout. Exploratory 6% position lets us test whether the fade thesis (headline fade → retracement) plays out. Confidence 3 (OVERDONE) warrants smaller sizing. What kills: actual large Switchblade/JUMP-20 contract or major Iran escalation (strikes, Strait closure)—either would justify the move and convert to UNDERDONE.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis that geopolitical spikes in defense mid-caps 'historically retrace 30-50% within two weeks' is asserted without citing a dataset or reference, making the fade assumption anecdotal rather than empirical.
red_team.critique: The setup is sound: 18% one-day move, 30.8% five-day move, 3.28x volume, with no 8-K or contract award disclosed—consistent with positioning rather than fundamental news. The exploratory 6% size fits a 3-confidence hypothesis test. However, the core fade thesis rests on an unverified historical pattern ('defense mid-caps historically retrace 30-50%'); absent a named study or verifiable data, this is pattern-matching rather than evidence. The what_kills criterion (actual contract or material escalation) is clear and testable, but the bull case for a fade relies on confidence in the retracement pattern itself, which is not grounded in provided data.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q2 earnings release (8-K filed 2026-05-28 with items 2.02 + 9.01)
entries.thesis: An ~36% single-day collapse on 8x volume following a 2.02 earnings 8-K, in a small semi-equipment supplier, is the signature of a genuine miss + guide cut, not an overreaction. The fact that the stock was already down ~32% over five days suggests informed selling preceded the print — the magnitude of the gap is consistent with a structural reset (guide cut, customer loss, or margin collapse) rather than a one-quarter blip. Hard to call OVERDONE without seeing the actual numbers; in photomask, customer concentration risk (large foundry/IDM orders) means a single lost program can reset the multiple permanently.
entries.what_kills: If the print shows only a modest miss with no guide change and the move is driven by a single forced seller or panic — then a snapback toward the pre-drawdown level becomes likely and OVERDONE would have been the right call
entries.reasoning: 36% one-day collapse on 8x volume following -31.7% five-day pre-print bleed is the signature of a real fundamental break (guide cut, customer loss, margin reset), not an overreaction to fade. RATIONAL classification at confidence 4 correctly identifies this as a genuine earnings miss, not OVERDONE. The thesis requires deep read of the 8-K exhibits (guide details, customer commentary, margin trajectory) before entry. Current setup does not present a directional edge—the gap likely reflects informed selling that already price the bad news. Will revisit after reviewing specific guide cut magnitude and customer concentration disclosures.

entries.name: Dick's Sporting Goods Inc
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings miss with Foot Locker integration costs hitting profitability
entries.thesis: 8-K Item 2.02 + 8.01 confirms earnings event; CNBC explicitly attributes the miss to Foot Locker turnaround costs eating profitability even as the top line surges. The drop reflects a real, structural margin concern from a known M&A overhang rather than a one-time blip. Magnitude looks appropriate given DKS was a quality compounder with premium multiple — when integration risk on a contentious deal actually shows up in numbers, multiple compression of this scale is normal.
entries.what_kills: Management call clarifies Foot Locker costs are front-loaded with clear exit ramp; legacy DKS comps remain strong and the miss was solely Foot Locker accounting — then fade-the-drop becomes viable
entries.reasoning: Earnings miss (-5.97% today) is RATIONAL at confidence 4: Foot Locker integration drag is real and structural, not a one-time blip. The drop reflects appropriate multiple compression on a quality compounder hit by known M&A overhang. Does not present a directional fade or buy opportunity at this moment—the question is whether Foot Locker costs are front-loaded (support reversion) or ongoing (justify further compression). Requires forward guidance review and sell-side commentary on integration timeline before entry. Current price action is consistent with the thesis; no edge yet.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; momentum extension against deteriorating mortgage backdrop
entries.thesis: BLDR has run sharply over the past week and added another leg today, but today's news flow includes mortgage refi demand dropping 18% as rates hit a nine-month high. That is a direct demand headwind for residential building products. The continuation move appears to be momentum-driven rather than fundamentally supported by the rate backdrop. Setup favors mean reversion if rates stay elevated.
entries.what_kills: Tape-level rate reversal lower; specific M&A or capital-return announcement from BLDR; homebuilder peers (DHI, LEN) breaking out alongside
entries.reasoning: Re-entry blocked: confidence is 3 (OVERDONE), which is below the confidence >= 4 re-entry bar. You closed BLDR 0 days ago at a gain; the flag's new thesis is momentum continuation into a worsening mortgage-rate backdrop (refi demand down 18%, rates at nine-month high). That is not genuinely new information—it is a continuation of the same rate/sector backdrop that drove the original UNDERDONE play. No company-specific catalyst, no macro reversal signal, no new structural data on housing demand. The setup (momentum fade into headwind) is legitimate on its own merits, but at confidence 3, re-entry violates the raised bar. Hold in WATCH; if the thesis sharpens with new data (starts miss, specific peer weakness) and confidence rises to 4+, revisit.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: 5/21 8-K with material agreement + new debt + unregistered equity issuance; today's price action is semi-sector tailwind.
entries.thesis: Today's move is mostly sector-driven (SMH +4.5%), but the 5/21 8-K bundling 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (financial obligation) + 3.02 (unregistered equity sales) + 8.01 looks like a financing/acquisition package. That combination most often signals an M&A deal funded with debt + stock issuance. Volume is actually below average today, so the catalyst is not driving incremental flow — the SMH tailwind is doing the work.
entries.what_kills: If the 8-K turns out to be a routine convertible note refinancing with no strategic element, the bundle is non-event and ONTO is purely a sector trade.
entries.reasoning: 8-K bundle (1.01 material agreement + 2.03 financial obligation + 3.02 unregistered equity) is a high-probability M&A-with-financing signature per your own learning note. Today's 4.55% move is pure SMH sector tailwind (semiconductor equipment +4.5%), not ONTO-specific flow (volume 0.89x average). Thesis is compelling but requires a press release or proxy to confirm deal structure, target, and size before entry. Confidence is 3 (RATIONAL), which is fair—the 8-K structure is strong signal but not confirmed transaction. Hold in WATCH pending deal announcement.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; profit-taking after weekly run alongside energy complex weakness on Iran de-escalation
entries.thesis: AMR ran hard into today and is pulling back as XLE drops on Iran-deal optimism. Met coal isn't oil but trades with broader commodity risk sentiment, and a give-back after a sharp run on no company-specific 8-K is the expected behavior. Magnitude looks proportionate — no directional edge.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K appears (contract, buyback, M&A) suggesting today's selloff was actually fundamental

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.thesis: AEIS is down with elevated volume but no filing, no earnings within window, and the broader semi tape (SMH -0.15%, NVDA -1.45%) doesn't fully explain the magnitude. Could be a sell-side downgrade, channel check, or sympathy with a specific customer — without that data point I can't articulate either side at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: News of a positive event being faded for unrelated reasons, or confirmation this is just position unwinding

entries.name: Manhattan Associates
entries.catalyst: Sector rotation into software/AI-adjacent names on XLK strength
entries.thesis: MANH is up on no specific catalyst, alongside a sharp XLK move driven by ORCL/MSFT/AVGO. Supply-chain software has tangential AI/agentic narrative pull. Magnitude looks consistent with sector beta plus mild momentum chase — no edge either way.
entries.what_kills: An 8-K or contract announcement appears suggesting today was fundamental, not beta

entries.name: Watsco, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possible HVAC/housing demand concern or peer-driven rotation
entries.thesis: WSO down ~4% in a tape where SPY is +0.55% and with no 8-K — but XLU -1.1% and rate-sensitive/housing-exposed names appear to be the losers today. Could be tied to housing/HVAC demand concerns or a sell-side note we can't see. Without a visible catalyst, can't articulate either bull (oversold/buy-the-dip) or bear (fundamental break) at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Confirms it's just sector rotation and snaps back tomorrow

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: Defense sector bid on Iran missile headlines
entries.thesis: Up ~14% on 2.5x volume, five-day +17% — strong but less extended than AVAV. Kratos has genuine ongoing CCA/drone program newsflow and the Iran tape provides a coherent reason. Magnitude is sharp but not yet at the extension level where a fade is high-conviction. Watch for whether this is following AVAV's setup or has its own contract catalyst.
entries.what_kills: If five-day pushes past 25%+ without contract news, fade setup becomes much more attractive

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No identified company catalyst; possibly oil-down/airline-up macro beta
entries.thesis: Stock is up sharply over five days with another leg today and no company-specific catalyst visible to us. Could be fuel cost tailwind from oil's collapse (Iran de-escalation), broader airline re-rating, or unreported analyst action. Without evidence I can't pick a direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Peer airlines flat or down while ALK rips alone strongly suggests unknown idiosyncratic news; if oil rebounds and ALK holds, the fuel narrative breaks

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
entries.thesis: BEAM has run sharply over five days with another large move today. XBI was up only modestly so this is largely idiosyncratic. Base editing readouts, partnership news, or conference data are the usual catalysts in this name but I don't have direct evidence of any.
entries.what_kills: Move is pure short-squeeze with no fundamental news — would fade on first market-wide risk-off day

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: 5/21 earnings 8-K, continuation move on risk-on tape
entries.thesis: DECK printed earnings via 8-K on 5/21 and has continued higher into today on above-average volume. Without the actual print details (guidance, HOKA/UGG splits) I cannot tell if the continuation is justified follow-through on a beat or short-covering overshoot in a quality compounder that had been beaten down. Both bull and bear cases require the segment data I don't have.
entries.what_kills: Either side requires the actual report contents to commit.

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No 8-K, no earnings within the window. Possibly sympathy with semi rotation or activist/M&A chatter (Starboard has historically held QRVO).
entries.thesis: Move has now compounded to a significant multi-day run without an 8-K or earnings event. Could be activist/M&A speculation (QRVO has been a recurring activist target), sympathy with broader semi rotation away from NVDA into smaller chip names (SMH strong, NVDA weak), or leaked sell-side note. Without a visible driver, direction-from-here is genuinely uncertain.
entries.what_kills: Discovery that the move is purely retail/momentum with no fundamental driver — would argue for fade. Alternatively, confirmed activist 13D would invalidate the 'no catalyst' framing entirely.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
entries.catalyst: May 21 8-K with 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure (content unknown from filing codes alone).
entries.thesis: BMI filed a 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure on May 21 and the stock has continued higher into today. Reg-FD filings can be either bullish (investor day reaffirming/raising outlook) or neutral (boilerplate conference deck). Without knowing the content of the filing, I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+. BMI is a low-beta water-meter compounder that rarely moves this much without substance, which slightly biases bullish, but not enough to commit.
entries.what_kills: The 7.01 is routine investor conference boilerplate — in which case the move is sentiment/sympathy with the utility/water infrastructure theme and could fade.

entries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown. Possibly networking sympathy or short squeeze.
entries.thesis: Volume multiple is below 1x (0.89), which is unusual for a sharp single-day move and weakens the conviction that institutional buyers are accumulating on news. Could be sympathy with networking peers (ANET, CSCO) on AI-networking theme but neither moved sharply today. No 8-K. The combination of sharp move + below-average volume + no visible catalyst is a yellow flag — could be a thin-tape squeeze rather than informed buying.
entries.what_kills: Volume stays light and the move fades over the next 1-2 sessions — classic thin-tape pop pattern.
